meta.name: kookuptable
meta.description: Etymological lookup table mapping modern English words to their ancestral root words. Each entry traces a word back to its deepest known historical root, honoring the original meaning the word carried before modern reinterpretation.

entries.root_meaning: father, protector, guardian
mother
brother, male kin of same generation
sister, female of one's own kind
daughter, the one who is milked or nourished
son, the one born
water, that which is wet
fire, the purifier
ground, soil, the world below
sun, the shining one
moon, the measurer (of time)
star, the strewn one (scattered across sky)
night, darkness
to burn, the burning time, the hot period
heart, the core, inner seat of feeling
to see, the seer
ear, the perceiver of sound
nose, nostril
to chew, the chewing place
tooth, the biting one, eater
tongue, the licker
hand, the seizer, the grasper
foot, that which treads
knee, the angle, the bend
bone, the hard inner part
blood, that which flows, sacred fluid
head, the top, the cup-shaped part
name, designation, that which is known by
to know, to recognize, to perceive
to see, to follow with the eyes, to perceive
to hear, to perceive sharply
to speak, to crackle, to make sounds
to eat, to consume, to devour
to draw in, to pull liquid, to suck
to sleep, to become limp, to slacken
to die, to become still, to cease
entries.pathway: PGmc *dawjaną → ON deyja → ME dien → die

need, compulsion, distress, necessity, constraint
lacking, absent, deficient — to want originally meant to lack
entries.pathway: PGmc *wanatą → ON vanta → ME wanten → want

entries.root_meaning: to wish, to desire, to strive for, to be content
to taste, to try, to relish — choosing was tasting/testing
to tread, to go, to walk — finding was what you came upon while walking
to seek, to search for, to follow a track
to hide, to conceal, to cover, to hoard
to look at, to observe, to show, to inspect
to watch, to guard, to stay awake waiting
to stand, to remain standing, to be fixed
entries.pathway: PIE *steh₂- → Lat stāre → OFr ester → stay

entries.root_meaning: to move, to set in motion, to stir, to remove
to stop up, to stuff, to plug with tow
entries.pathway: Lat stuppāre (from stuppa 'tow') → OE stoppian → stop

entries.root_meaning: to tumble, to stumble, to leap up suddenly — to start was to jump
to reach, to stretch out, to extend the hand
to grasp, to grip, to seize with the hand
drop, a falling globule, that which drips
to step, to stamp, to tread firmly
to leap, to run, to spring forward
to jump, to spring up, to bound
to stretch, to draw along — dancing as drawing oneself along rhythmically
rest, repose, a distance of rest on a journey (a league), stopping place
to fasten, to bind — peace as the binding compact between enemies
entries.pathway: PIE *pak- → Lat pāx → OFr pais → peace

entries.root_meaning: still, motionless, fixed, standing without movement
rest, repose, calm, the state of being at rest
entries.pathway: Lat quiētus (at rest) → OFr quiet → quiet

entries.root_meaning: loud, famous, heard far away, renowned
wild, untamed, self-willed, natural, not controlled
to tame, to domesticate, to subdue, to overpower
to rule, to direct — rich originally meant powerful, kingly
producing little, having little (paucus 'little' + parāre 'to produce')
that which is owed, what must be given back
entries.pathway: Lat dēbitum (from dēbēre 'to owe') → OFr dette → debt

entries.root_meaning: to own, to possess — to owe is to have a possession-obligation
to fasten, to fix — to pay was to pacify, to settle a debt, to make peace
entries.pathway: PIE *pak- → Lat pācāre (to pacify) → OFr paier → pay

entries.root_meaning: worth, value, price, that which is turned/exchanged
to stand together, to stand firm, to be fixed at a price
dear, precious, valuable, worthy, beloved
purchase, bargain, trade — cheap originally meant a good purchase
trade, buying, the place of trading
entries.pathway: Lat mercātus (from merx 'goods') → OE market → market

entries.root_meaning: army-shelter, the lodging for warriors, safe haven
sail, the cloth that catches wind for travel
oar, that which moves through water, the water-mover
anchor, the bent hook, that which holds fast by bending
entries.pathway: Grk ánkyra → Lat ancora → OE ancor → anchor

entries.root_meaning: time, season, hour — the tide was a division of time
shore, the cut edge of land, where land is cut by water
cliff, steep rock face, that which rises sheer
hollow, empty, the hollow space inside earth
den, floor, threshing floor, flat ground, animal's lair
nest, the sitting-place (ni- 'down' + sed- 'to sit')
egg, the oval life-container
entries.pathway: PGmc *ajją → ON egg → ME eg → egg

entries.root_meaning: to fly, to rush — feathers are the instruments of flight
wing, the wind-catcher, that which rides the wind
entries.pathway: PGmc *wehngaz → ON vængr → ME winge → wing

entries.root_meaning: tail, hair, rope-like appendage
horn, head, top, the hard growth on the head
claw, the hooking nail, the scratcher
fang, the seizer, the catching tooth (from 'to catch')
stag, male deer, the pointed/horned one
fox, the bushy-tailed one, the thick-haired
raven, the croaking one, the dark screamer
hawk, the seizer, the grasping bird
eagle, the dark-colored bird, the swarthy one
owl, the howler, the wailing night-bird
worm, serpent, the turning/twisting creature
to crawl, to creep, to sneak — snake is the creeping one
spinner, the one who spins thread (from web-spinning)
bee, the buzzing one, the trembling insect
fly, the flying insect, the winged one
ant, the cutting one, the biter-off
frog, the leaper, the hopper
mouse, the stealer, the little thief
cat, the hunting companion (possibly from late Latin cattus)
entries.pathway: Lat cattus → PGmc *kattuz → OE catt → cat

entries.root_meaning: pig, the young swine, the pointed-snout one
hen, the singing one, she who chants (from 'to sing')
cock, rooster, the one who crows at dawn
ox, bull, the sprinkler (one who sprinkles seed/water while plowing)
mare, female horse
colt, young animal, the frisky young one
lamb, young sheep, the small weak one
